# Progress Details ‚Äî 03-ltcsharp-retarget

## Summary
`LTCSharp\LTCSharp.vcxproj` successfully retargeted from .NET Framework 4.8 to .NET 10 C++/CLI. Both Debug and Release configurations build and produce a `net10.0-windows` managed assembly.

## Changes Made

### LTCSharp\LTCSharp.vcxproj
- `TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>` ‚Üí `TargetFramework>net10.0-windows</TargetFramework>`
- `CLRSupport>true</CLRSupport>` ‚Üí `CLRSupport>NetCore</CLRSupport>` (both Debug and Release configs)
- Removed `<Reference Include="System" />`, `<Reference Include="System.Data" />`, `<Reference Include="System.Xml" />` ‚Äî no longer needed; SDK provides .NET BCL automatically in .NET Core C++/CLI mode
- `libltc` project reference retained unchanged

### LTCSharp\AssemblyInfo.cpp
- Removed `using namespace System::Security::Permissions` ‚Äî namespace removed in .NET 5+
- Removed `[assembly:SecurityPermission(SecurityAction::RequestMinimum, UnmanagedCode = true)]` ‚Äî `SecurityAction::RequestMinimum` was removed in .NET 5+

### LTCSharp\Classes.h
- Removed `override`/`new` specifiers from `IFormattable::ToString(String^, ...)` overloads ‚Äî C4490: MSVC C++/CLI does not allow `override`/`new` on interface implementations (only ref class base methods qualify); removing the specifier is correct and matches the C# equivalent of `public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider provider)`
- Added empty struct body definitions for opaque `LTCDecoder` and `LTCEncoder` types after `#include "ltc.h"` ‚Äî resolves LNK4248 "unresolved typeref token" warnings from the C++/CLI MSIL linker needing type metadata for native pointer handles

### LTCSharp\Classes.cpp
- Replaced all five `#pragma mark <Section>` directives with `// --- Section ---` comments ‚Äî `#pragma mark` is Apple Clang-specific and triggers C4068 "unknown pragma" on MSVC
- `sprintf(...)` ‚Üí `sprintf_s(...)` ‚Äî fixes C4996 unsafe function warning
- `strcpy(...)` ‚Üí `strcpy_s(...)` ‚Äî fixes C4996 unsafe function warning
- Added `default: return LTC_TV_625_50;` to `Utils::toNative(TVStandard)` switch ‚Äî fixes C4715 not-all-control-paths-return-a-value warning

## Build Results

**Debug|Win32**: ‚úÖ Succeeds ‚Äî LTCSharp.dll (.NET 10 managed assembly)
**Release|Win32**: ‚úÖ Succeeds ‚Äî LTCSharp.dll (.NET 10 managed assembly)

## Remaining Warnings (pre-existing, not fixable in this task)

| Warning | Source | Reason |
|---------|--------|--------|
| LNK4075 | `libltc.lib(timecode.obj)` | libltc was compiled with `/EDITANDCONTINUE`; C++/CLI forces `/INCREMENTAL:NO`. Would require recompiling libltc. No runtime impact. |
| C4244 (multiple) | `libltc\src\*.c` | Double‚Üíint/size_t truncation in the third-party native C library. Pre-existing, out of scope. |
| C4996 in libltc | `libltc\src\timecode.c` | `strcpy`/`sprintf` in the third-party C library. Pre-existing, out of scope. |

## Files Modified
- `LTCSharp/LTCSharp.vcxproj` ‚Äî CLRSupport, TargetFramework, reference cleanup
- `LTCSharp/AssemblyInfo.cpp` ‚Äî removed SecurityPermission and Security.Permissions namespace
- `LTCSharp/Classes.h` ‚Äî ToString specifier fix, LNK4248 struct stubs
- `LTCSharp/Classes.cpp` ‚Äî pragma mark replacement, sprintf_s/strcpy_s, default return

## Done When Verification
- [x] `LTCSharp.vcxproj` builds successfully targeting `net10.0-windows` (Debug and Release)
- [x] The managed C++/CLI assembly is a .NET 10 MSIL module (`.NETCoreApp,Version=v10.0.AssemblyAttributes.cpp` generated by MSBuild confirms this)
- [x] `libltc` native dependency links correctly (libltc.lib ‚Üí LTCSharp.dll)
- [x] No new warnings introduced beyond pre-existing libltc C4244/C4996/LNK4075
